title: GCP IAM resource hierarchy and policy inheritance
description: Explains how allow policies set at org/folder/project inherit downward, how the union of bindings applies, and the role of deny policies and conditions.
body: Use when reasoning about effective GCP permissions. Do not assume a permission must be granted at the resource itself. Actions: remember the hierarchy is org->folder->project->resource and allow policies inherit downward, the effective access is the union of all inherited bindings, conditions can narrow a binding, and IAM deny policies override allows. Verify with the Policy Analyzer. Validation signals include the analyzer attributing access to a specific inherited binding and a deny policy blocking as expected. Risk boundary: bindings high in the hierarchy have wide blast radius and warrant human review.
